pi_items.source: task.json description matches exactly: 'Create three new folders named exactly KB-Security, KB-Linux, and KB-Development'
verifier.py line 80: checks 'if count >= 3' and awards minimum population points; export_result.sh counts emails in each folder
task.json description: 'aim for at least 5 emails per folder'; verifier.py line 95: checks total_kb_emails >= 15 (5 per folder average)
task.json description: 'Draft an email to it-team@company.com'; export_result.sh line 105 searches for 'it-team@company.com'; verifier.py line 126 checks recipient_match
task.json description: 'Create a text file at ~/Documents/kb_index.txt'; export_result.sh line 18: INDEX_FILE = os.path.join(DOCS_DIR, 'kb_index.txt') where DOCS_DIR = '/home/ga/Documents'
setup_task.sh line 36-46: loads 50 ham emails from /workspace/assets/emails/ham; verified directory contains exactly 50 ham_001.eml through ham_050.eml files
Email headers show spamassassin.taint.org domain and 2002 timestamps; search confirmed SpamAssassin public corpus at https://spamassassin.apache.org/old/publiccorpus/

pi_items.verified_value: real email samples from public corpus
pi_items.source: Examined ham_001.eml and ham_002.eml headers - contain real sender addresses, recipients, mailing list messages from 2002. SpamAssassin corpus documented as containing 1897 spam and 4150 ham messages from a public research corpus

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real email data from the Apache SpamAssassin public corpus (2002), containing 50 legitimate technical emails in the inbox and 20 spam emails in junk. The agent must create exactly three folders (KB-Security, KB-Linux, KB-Development), move at least 3 emails per folder (targeting 5 per folder for optimal score), create an index file at /home/ga/Documents/kb_index.txt documenting the categorization, and send an announcement to it-team@company.com. Pass threshold is 65 points verified by verifier.py::verify_knowledge_base_extraction. All metadata claims are consistent with task implementation.
